anchor.snippet: states that the Boolean satisfiability problem is NP-complete

annotations.label: Polynomial SAT algorithm implies P = NP

anchor.snippet: if there exists a deterministic polynomial-time algorithm for solving Boolean satisfiability
there exists an oracle A such that, for all subexponential deterministic-time complexity classes T
he found for each of these problems an algorithm that solves it in optimal time
A decision problem is in NP if it can be decided by a nondeterministic Turing machine in polynomial time
An instance of the Boolean satisfiability problem is a Boolean expression that combines Boolean variables using Boolean operators
Such an expression is satisfiable if there is some assignment of truth values to the variables
SAT is in NP because any assignment of Boolean values to Boolean variables that is claimed to satisfy the given expression can be verified in polynomial time

annotations.label: Every NP problem reduces to SAT

anchor.snippet: every NP problem can be reduced to an instance of a SAT problem by a polynomial-time many-one reduction

annotations.label: Construction of Boolean expression satisfiable iff machine accepts

anchor.snippet: Thus the transformation is certainly a polynomial-time many-one reduction
proving that there exists a problem (the recognition of true quantified Boolean formulas) that is PSPACE-complete
proving that there exists a problem that is NL-complete

annotations.label: NP reduces to SAT in logarithmic space

anchor.snippet: every problem in NP can be reduced in polynomial time (in fact, logarithmic space suffices) to an instance of the Boolean satisfiability problem
21 diverse combinatorial and graph theoretical problems , each infamous for its intractability, are NP-complete
he showed the problem 3SAT (the Boolean satisfiability problem for expressions in conjunctive normal form (CNF) with exactly three variables or negations of variables per clause) to be NP-complete
